category_title: Students with SEBD who respond to peer provocation with reactive aggression and 'payback' behaviors
scenario_title: Attempting to report peer bullying to a teacher
minimal_context: The student is meeting one-on-one with a teacher in the office to report a peer conflict that just occurred.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You asked to speak with me about what happened with the other students.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I get angry when people are annoying me. They were calling me names and hurting me, because they know they can get to me.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, well, we will touch on this later.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: If someone hurt me, like kicked me or something, you wouldn't do anything about it. I would overreact and start going off to them and trying to kick them.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to calm down right now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know that the teachers think that I'm bad, so they'll just go for their side. I don't bother to say anything.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: You asked to speak with me about what happened with the other students.

STUDENT: I get angry when people are annoying me. They were calling me names and hurting me, because they know they can get to me.

TUTOR: Okay, well, we will touch on this later.

STUDENT: If someone hurt me, like kicked me or something, you wouldn't do anything about it. I would overreact and start going off to them and trying to kick them.

TUTOR: You need to calm down right now.

STUDENT: I know that the teachers think that I'm bad, so they'll just go for their side. I don't bother to say anything.
